response:
The fare calculation relies on a base fee plus extra charges for distance beyond the limit.
spring brings new hope to the weary driver who works long hours.
spring also reminds us that every journey has a unique cost structure.
The relationship between fare and distance is defined by a clear mathematical formula.
If the distance exceeds four kilometers then the price increases by two yuan per unit.
This formula allows us to calculate the total cost for any given trip length.
We must account for the time spent waiting as equivalent distance in the final sum.
The total fare depends on both the physical distance and the converted waiting time.
The maximum stopping time can be found by subtracting the base distance from the total.
Since the actual distance was fifteen kilometers the extra distance comes from waiting.
The fare of thirty eight yuan implies a specific total distance of twenty two kilometers.
Subtracting the fifteen kilometers of actual driving leaves seven kilometers of waiting time.
Each kilometer of waiting time represents five minutes so the total is thirty five minutes.
This means the taxi stopped for at most thirty five minutes during the entire trip.
